annotations.decision: Baby girl [**Known lastname 69778**] was a 2.3 kg product of a 36 and 2/7 weeks gestation born to a 35-year-old G3, P1, now 2 woman.
Notable for depression. Not on medication.
A positive, direct Coombs' negative, hepatitis surface antigen negative, RPR nonreactive, rubella immune, GBS unknown.
Antenatal history was complicated by IUGR
underwent repeat cesarean section under spinal anesthesia
no interpartum fever
Rupture of membranes occurred at delivery and yielded clear amniotic fluid
did not receive interpartum antibiotic prophylaxis
The infant was vigorous at delivery, orally and nasally suctioned, dried
Tactile stimulus
Apgars were 8 at 1 minute, and 8 at 5 minutes.
The infant was noted to have grunting with respirations
transferred to the newborn intensive care unit for further evaluation.
Birth weight was 2.3 kg, 25th to 50th percentile; head circumference 30.5 cm, 10th percentile; length 46.25 cm, 25th to 50th percentile.
Anterior fontanel soft and flat. Nondysmorphic.
Palate intact. Neck and mouth normal. No nasal flaring.
Red reflex normal bilaterally
Chest with mild intercostal retractions. Good breaths sounds bilaterally. No adventitious sounds.
Well perfused. Regular rate and rhythm. Femoral pulses normal. S1 and S2 normal. No murmurs.
Soft, nondistended. No organomegaly. No masses. Bowel sounds active. Anus patent. Three vessel umbilical cord.
Normal external female genitalia.
The infant is active, responding to stimuli. Tone mildly decreased in symmetrical distribution
Moves all extremities symmetrically. Suck, root, gag present and normal. Normal spine with limbs, hips and clavicles.
placed on CPAP for management of respiratory distress
respiratory distress
The infant transitioned to room air by day of life No. 1 and has been stable in room air since that time.
The infant has been cardiovascularly stable with a new onset murmur
seen by TCH cardiology service
The cardiology service felt the murmur was consistent with a small VSD.
60 cc per kg per day of D10W
The infant has been euglycemic throughout hospital course.
phototherapy
Her rebound bili after phototherapy stopped was 6.9/0.2.
CBC was benign and blood cultures remained negative at 48 hours
ampicillin and gentamycin were discontinued.
The infant has been appropriate for gestational age.
Audiology hearing screen has been performed with automated auditory brain stem responses and the infant passed in the right ear but referred in the left ear.
Stable

Feeds at discharge: Continue ad lib feeding breast milk     or Similac 20 calorie
.  Car seat position screening was performed.  The infant     was screened with 90-minute screening in the car seat and     the infant passed.
State newborn screens have been sent per protocol and     have been within normal limits.
The infant received Hep B vaccine     on [**8-30**].
Prematurity at 36 2/7 weeks gestation
Mild respiratory distress syndrome
Hyperbilirubinemia
Rule out sepsis with antibiotics
antibiotics
